assessments.threshold: No inherent corrosion protection; outdoor exposure common
assessments.rationale: Industrial door guide rails are frequently exposed to outdoor weather, loading dock environments, and chemical wash-down. Bare steel delivery requires external hot-dip galvanizing (EN ISO 1461) adding 2-4 weeks to lead time.
assessments.mitigation: External hot-dip galvanizing (EN ISO 1461) or zinc-rich primer; planned adjacency: offer galvanized-as-delivered to reduce gap vs roll-formed (pre-galvanized coil)

assessments.rationale: Aluminium extruded door profiles account for ~10% of market (Hydro, Constellium). ZOLLERN cannot serve aluminium door segment. Steel door systems (~90%) are fully addressable.
assessments.mitigation: Focus on steel sliding, fire-rated, security, and industrial door segments (90% of addressable market)

assessments.threshold: 6-12 weeks for new guide rail geometry
assessments.rationale: New door model programs have 6-12 month development cycles; die lead fits within product development timeline. Repeat geometry orders ship within days.
assessments.mitigation: Die library of common T-slot and guide rail cross-sections; early engagement with door OEM engineering

assessments.rationale: Industrial door guide rail orders typically exceed 100 kg per batch. Standard orders are 200-1000 kg for production runs.

assessments.threshold: Seal groove tolerance +-0.05 mm for intumescent strip compression
assessments.rationale: ZOLLERN cold-drawn profiles achieve +-0.02 mm on seal groove dimensions - well within +-0.05 mm EN 1634 requirement. This is actually a ZOLLERN advantage vs roll-forming (+-0.2 mm).
